office_does: County commissioners set county policy and budgets, oversee departments, and represent the county on regional bodies.
race_blurb: Incumbent Gunner Fulmer faces Rikki Wescott and Troy Woody; Woody proposes a structural charter overhaul while Wescott emphasizes public services and state funding.

candidates.summary: Local business owner proposing a charter-county restructuring of county government.
highlights: Proposes professional administration and five lower-paid part-time commissioners [S2]
Would convert five technical elected offices to appointed roles and lower operating costs [S2]

spending.basis: The platform explicitly seeks lower county operating costs through structural consolidation and lower-paid commissioners.

experience.basis: A business-owner challenger proposing wholesale structural change presents a renewal-oriented profile.

reform.basis: Charter-county conversion, a professional administrator, and replacement of the current elected-office structure are explicit structural reforms.

candidates.summary: Incumbent commissioner with county, regional transportation, and statewide county-association responsibilities.
highlights: Former police and fire sergeant with more than 27 years of service [S2]
Serves in regional transportation and statewide county-association roles [S2][S3]

experience.basis: Incumbency plus extensive public-safety and intergovernmental service establish a maximal proven-record profile.

candidates.summary: Management and financial-analysis professional emphasizing services, infrastructure, health care, and state funding.
highlights: Background includes financial planning, management, organizing, and advocacy [S2]
Emphasizes roads, EMS, schools, health care, community centers, and obtaining state funds [S2]
Also identifies small-business reuse and ecological protection [S2]

spending.basis: The platform emphasizes multiple public services and obtaining state funding as the principal lever.

climate.basis: Ecological protection is an explicit priority, but the dossier gives no detail on climate or energy policy.
